summary: Transcendent Here Comes Sunshine & powerhouse Other One jam
blurb: The show shines with a soaring Here Comes Sunshine and a thunderous The Other One sequence. Jerry’s guitar is on fire while Phil delivers deep, experimental bass explorations. Highlights include the Truckin' → Nobody's Fault But Mine → The Other One segue and strong renditions of Stella Blue and Casey Jones.
review: December 19, 1973 at Curtis Hixon Convention Hall captured one of the band’s late‑’73 peaks. From the opening run through a blistering Here Comes Sunshine, the first set builds steady momentum, punctuated by a fiery Candyman and a solid Playing In The Band that sets up the second‑set fireworks. The second half erupts with a seamless He's Gone > Truckin' > Nobody's Fault But Mine > The Other One chain, launching into an expansive jam that rolls into an inspired Stella Blue and closes with the joyous energy of Around & Around and Casey Jones. Jerry Garcia’s lead work is described as “transcendent” on Sunshine and “soaring” throughout the Truckin' segues, while Phil Lesh’s bass provides low‑frequency explosions and a Decon‑style jam that foreshadows later experimental forays. Keith Godchaux adds ethereal chordal textures and a few prickly bluesy stingers, and Bill Kreutzmann’s snare work on Big Railroad Blues shines as a rhythmic anchor. Overall, the performance is consistently praised across all recordings, making this concert a definitive example of the band’s late‑’73 chemistry.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All seven recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the Here Comes Sunshine jam, the Truckin' → Nobody's Fault But Mine → The Other One sequence, and strong individual performances (Jerry’s guitar, Phil’s bass, Keith’s keys). Consistent high‑energy language across sources justifies a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent two‑track soundboard from Dick’s Picks, crisp and well‑balanced with no dropouts; captures the transcendent Here Comes Sunshine jam and standout versions of Candyman, Dire Wolf, and Truckin'.

key_highlights: Soaring Here Comes Sunshine that sets the tone for the night
Thunderous The Other One sequence following a legendary Truckin' → Nobody's Fault But Mine segue
Keith’s “ridiculous” outro keys after Truckin' add extra sparkle
Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with no audible flaws

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers transcendent, genius guitar moments; his lead on Here Comes Sunshine and the Truckin' segues is described as on fire and perfectly driving the jams.
band_performance.Phil: Provides deep, experimental bass work with low‑frequency explosions and a standout Decon‑style jam that fuels the second‑set peaks.
band_performance.Bob: Solid vocal contributions throughout; occasional over‑the‑top screaming noted on Around & Around but generally supportive of the band’s energy.
band_performance.Billy: Snare work shines on Big Railroad Blues and Big River, offering a tight rhythmic foundation that propels the high‑energy moments.
